As a soule in mortal sinne spiritually is dead, so truly repenting it returneth to spiritual life. Not only repentance, which is auersion from sinne, & conuersion to God, but also doing penance, that is satisfaction for sinnes is necessary to saluation.
In that the Chaldees were the ministers of Gods wrath, they are called his rod, as Assur is called the rod of his furie. Isa. 10. v. 5.
By the nose S. Ierom, vnderstandeth the King; by the eares the Priests & Iudges, who were not slaine but cut off from the people, and caried into captiuitie. 4. Kg. 25. Iere. 52.
This demand is an admonition to iudge & condemne their wickednes, without intermission.
Circumstances of places and times doe aggrauate sinnes very much, which are therefore more seuerely punished.
Of this place S. Ierom proueth that God rewardeth Infidels for their moral good works temporally, though they cannot merite an eternal reward as the just doe.
The day of iust punishment is called the day of our Lord. As the Apostle calleth the day of general iudgement the day of our Lord. 1. Cor. 5. 2. Cor. 1. & 1. Thess. 1.
Your captiuitie and distressed state hath giuen occasion to al nations to speake & discourse of you. As Ieremie prophesied, ch. 24. v. 9, I wil giue them into reproch, and a Prouerb &c.

In explicating this vision by reason of the obscuritie, is great varietie amongst the expositours, but al agree that God here reuealed to the Prophet, that he wil reward the good, to wit, in the old Testament temporally, & in the new spiritually, first with grace in this life, and with eternal glorie in life euerlasting.

To the gates of the vtter wal was ascent of seuen steps, but of the inner wal (v. 21.) of eight steps, to signifie that more perfection is required in the new Testament then was in the old, for which more grace is giuen and better reward. S. Gregorie.
The coniunctiō, And, being the first word, either ioyneth the context to the title, or (as S. Gregorie expoundeth it) the exteriour words vttered to the interiour, reuealed to the Prophet in spirit.
Either the thirtith yeare of his age, or the 30. since the captiuitie was Prophecied, in the reigne of Iosias 4.Reg. 22. v.18.
They turned not about, but hauing faces on euery side were readie to goe euerie way.

S. Gregorie expounding this vision of the foure Euangelistes, or of the whole new Testament, teacheth that the old & new Testaments are each in the other; both teaching the same things in diuers manners.

In this chapter the Prophet describeth foure parts of a vision which he saw at one time: of a whirle winde, v.4. of foure liuing creatures, v.5. of foure wheeles v.15. & of a man sitting in a throne in the firmament. v.26. For vnderstanding of al which, learned Commentaries doe hardly suffice.

Sonne of man, but no other so calleth him. Here the Angel most frequently calleth Ezechiel the Sonne of man, as wel to distinguish between Angelical and humane kind, as in honour of Christ, of whom this and other Prophets were figures: but why Ezechiel, and scarce any other (Dan.8. v.17.) had this title is hard to explicate.
Lamentation, and song to the praises of God.
Woe to the desperate.
To make a banke, a ditch is also made, ordinarily about three foot deep, & the earth cast vp towards the towne besieged; with couert passage about seuen foot in height.
It is very hard to explicate, how the ten tribes were 190. yeares in captiuitie.
And from what time the 40. yeares are counted, seeing it is certaine, that the two tribes were in captiuitie 70. yeares. S. Ierome in Ezech.
As a staffe aydeth the weake, so bread susteyneth al men.
In other cities of Iuda neere Ierusalem.
Ieremie, Lament. 2. and Baruch ch. 2. prophecied of the same distresse by famine in the siege of Ierusalem.
Blasphemie is here taken improperly, & signifieth that other nations shal not only reproch Ierusalem, but also taunt & scoffe at her miseries.
Idolatrie was most especially committed in mountaines, or hils, & therfore both idols and idolaters were destroyed in the same places.
Gods perpetual prouidence stil preservueth some relikes that serue him sincerely, and before those depart from this world.
Others doe repent and returne to God.
In this vision appeared in the ayre as it were a man sitting in a throne of sapphire stone, ouer the image of Cherubs, & foure wheels vnder them.
The strange forme of these foure payre of wheels, signified the consonant agreement of the old and new Testament. S.Greg. ho.6. in Ezech.
They were readie to goe forward, backward, on the right hand, on the left, or to what part foeuer without turning about.

It seemeth euerie one had as it were foure faces: al like mans faces, but one more resembling a mans face then the other, one somewhat resembling an oxe face (ch.1.) here called the face of a Cherub, another the face of a lion, the other an eagle.
The Prophet in Chaldea saw in spirit what was done in Ierusalem.
Were not new houses builded (say the false Prophets) since Ieremie said, that al our houses should be destroyed? whereupon they inferre that his Prophecie is false.
And so cornered themselues as secure, in Ierusalem: as flesh in the Potte.
The false Prophets scattered warres, but not captiuitie, therfore the Prophet assureth them that the people shal feele both sword & captiuitie.
He lamenteth not the death of the false Prophet, but feared great ruine of the people, seeing this wicked man dye so sodainly.
God stil conservueth his Church from vtter ruine, as the Prophets doe often affirme. Iere. 4 v. 17, ch. 5. v 10. 18. Psal. 88. v. 3. 35.
As a wal of clay, or morter without straw or other temperature, is washed away with rayne, so vaine hopes of securitie, without repentance & good workes deceiue the careles people that liue in sinne.

There were also false Prophetisses feyning to be illuminated with the spirit of Prophecie; by flatterie deceiued the people, saying they were in good state & in securitie, when they were in sinne, & in extreme danger of both temporal and eternal ruine.

God reuealed to the Prophet that these men came not sincerely to learne, but were setled in their harts to serue the idols. As worldly men in heretical countries doe inquire of Catholike Priests what they should doe, but remaine resolued to participate with heretikes.
Such men are first of al to be admonished to depart from idolatrie, heresie, schisme, & from al practice thereof: which is the first step of true conuersion to God.
God permitteth false Prophets to be deceiued, & to deceiue, in punishment of their great sinnes, and of the people that heare them. 3. Reg. 22. v 19. 2. Par. 18. v. 18.
Daniel then liuing, Noe & Iob departed from this life did sometime pray for the people.
Noe is named for example of spiritual Pastours of the Church, Daniel of al religious orders, & Iob of holy laie people. S. Greg. l.1. c.1.
There shal alwayes be Pastours to bring forth & feede spiritual children of God.

Gods Church is very often and fitly compared to a vine in respect of the excellent fruit: & so the branches cut off from the Church, are most like to wilde superfluous vine, good for nothing but the fire. S. Aug. Tract. 7. 81. in Io.
In burning a fagot the middles being first consumed, the rest of each ends are likewise put in the fire til al be consumed: so none that are out of the Church can escape the fire.
Ezechiel was now in Babylon, & therfore this admonition which he should giue to Ierusalem was to be notified there by letters, & messengers sent thither for this purpose.

By al this is signified that God made the Israelites of a barbarous nation to be ciuil, and gaue them not only things necessarie, but also ornaments, aboue the state of other nations, especially in spiritual benefites, giuing a Law, with Sacrifices, Sacraments, and other holy rites.
Adulterous women doe deceiue their husbands, bringing them other mens children: but the Iewes gaue their lawful children to the worst adulterers, sacrificing them to idols. 4. Reg. 6.17.21.23.
Al fornication is abominable, but that is most detestable when women giue rewards to men for fornication or adultrie.
As Ierusalem was wont to be wicked in former times, so it is now.
Ambition, gluttonie, & idlenes are cause of much more sinnes; temperance & labour bring forth much good fruit.
By Sodom & other cities are vnderstood al nations, which shal come to Christ.
After that al other nations are conuerted the Iewes shal also returne to Christ. Ro. 11.
Al gentils elder & yonger shal be conuerted to Christ.
Not by the couenant made with the Iewes in the Law of Moyses, but by the Euangelical couenant.
Nabuchodonosor like an eagle rauenous to the praye, & speedie in seizing thereon, spoyled the vineyard (signifying Ierusalem) notwithstanding they sought help of another eagle, the King of Aegypt.
The explication of the parable.
The King of Babylon made King Sedecias King of Iuda, tooke of him an oath of allegiance to be vnder him, which oath Sedecias breaked, and was therefore iustly plagued.
Nabuchodonosor planted a Kingdome vnder Sedecias, which prospered not: but Gods Church planted by Christ shal neuer faile.
Ierusalem brought forth cruell Kings, as Ioachaz, Ioakim, Iechonias, and Sedecias. 4. Reg. 21. & 24.
Rouers of Chaldea, of Syria, of Moab, & of Ammon. 4. Reg. 24. v. 2.
The Kingdom of al the Israelites was planted and prospered vnder Dauid and Salomon, but decayed vnder other Kings: & first ten tribes, afterwards the other two were carried in captiuitie.

Ezechiel began to Prophecie in the fifth yeare after the transmigration of Iechonias; after which he was silent 390. dayes and 40. more. Now in the sixth yeare in the fifth month tenth day, the elders comming to demand Prophecie it was reuealed to him not to answere.
According to their deserts al should haue been destroyed, but God for his names sake saued his people, as he had promised.
God lifted vp his hand either to deliuer, or to threaten and punish.

By long custome of Sacrificing to idols vpon the Excelses (high places), the false name was vsed by euill custome; as heretikes do commonly vse termes belonging to heresie: as Table for Altar, Seruice for Sacrifice.
God wil rather haue idolaters to leaue him wholy than to halt between two. 3. Reg. 18.
Such doe especially pollute Gods name. Rom. 2. v. 24.
Temporal afflictions fal also vpon the iust; not for punishment, but for their merite.
The whole people of Israel was the peculiar adopted sonne of God, called also his first begotten, Exo. 4. v. 22.
The Babylonians consulting the idols whether they should inuade the Ammonites or the Iewes, were directed to assault Ierusalem.
The Ammonites reproaching the Iewes when they were afflicted by the Babylonians, were for the same cause destroyed also by the Babylonians.
Why dost thou cease from admonishing & blaming the people for their bloody sinnes?

A merueilous force of iust mens praiers, by which God suffereth himselfe to be ouercome. And it is most grateful to God when some doe put themselues in opposition to his furie against sinners, that they may repent. S. Iero.

Ieremie in Babylon saw what was done the same time in Ierusalem, & therfore is bid to write the day, that thereby the certaintie of his prophecie may appeare. For the verie same day Nabuchodonosor layde siege to Ierusalem. 4. Reg. 25. v. 1.
When sinners are not amended by tribulation, God after they are parted from this world, punisheth them euerlastingly.

The suddaine death of nere freinds causeth more sorow, then if it were feared before: yet the Prophet was commanded not to shew sorow for the suddaine death of his wife, to signifie that the great calamitie of euerie one would take away the particular griefe for the losse of priuate freinds.
Amongst other heathen nations, especially the Ammonites reioyced at the miseries of the Iewes, & were therfore plagued.
The Chaldees, or rather the Armenians or Agarens (who are more direct eastward, and were Pastours of cattle) subdued the Ammonites.
God wil take away al the strength and force of Moab, which consisteth in his strong cities.
Yeares are stil counted from the transmigration of Ioachin.
Tyre which is a most frequented sea towne, shal be made desolate and to no vse but to hang nets therein to drie.
Cities and townes perteyning to Tyre shal likewise be wasted and destroyed.

As Tyre was exalted in pride, so it was brought to great ruine, yet was it restored after; according to Isaias prophecie ch. 23. v. 15. And our Sauiour retired sometimes into the quarters of Tyre and Sidon. Mat. 15. v. 21.
This large description of the Tyrians glory sheweth their greater ruine.

S. Ierome translating this word a iasper stone, and the Septuagints leaue it vntranslated; neither doe the Hebrew Doctors describe it in their commentaries. The Chaldee Paraphrase translateth it in general precious stones.

Cutting of haire was a signe of sorow, which the Iewes were commanded not to maintaine. Deut. 14. to shew difference from other nations: yet they also did cut their haire in great calamities. Isa. 21. v. 12.
Daniel was so famous for wisedome, that the prouerbe in Chaldea was to compare wise men with Daniel.
Tyre had much iniquity long before, but when she thought herselfe to be God, this iniquity could not be longer tolerated.

Thou that seemest to thyselfe inuincible, yet art thou not equal to the King of Assirians, who is already ouerthrowne; and so shalt thou likewise be.

Here it is manifest that this Prophet counteth the yeares by the time of transmigration of King Iechonias, for it is cleere that Sedecias reigned only eleuen yeares. 4. Reg. 24. & 25. Iere. 39. & 52.
This hyperbolical speach describeth the former glorie of Ægypt, as if al lights were much diminished, when this Kingdome was darkned.
Pastours are not excused omitting to admonish their flock either for feare of danger, or for despaire of the sinners amendment.
Gods absolute iustice or consequent wil is alwayes fulfilled: but not his conditionall or antecedent, as is noted ch. 18. v. 21.
God being alwayes ready to shew mercie, knocking at the dore of our hart, it is of mens owne wilfulnes that they are not saued. S. Tho. 1. 2. q. 109. a. 6.
As he Prophecied ch. 24. v. 2. the very day when the siege began: so he foreshewed also that one flying away should tel of the taking & spoiling of the citie three yeares after.

He that hath spiritual charge of his flock, & seeketh his owne temporal profite, not the spiritual good of his flock, is indeed no Pastour, but a hireling; or if he also teach false doctrine, he is a woolfe.

Not only Christ himselfe but also others shal doe the right Offices of true Pastours in the new Testament, as S. Paul teacheth Ephe. 4. that God giueth Apostles, Prophets, Euangelists, Pastours & doctors, to the consummation of Saints, til we meete al in the vnitie of faith.

No Iew is so obstinate, nor heretike so blind, but al confesse that the Messias Christ, is here called by the name of Dauid. For King Dauid was now dead long before this Prophet liued. See also ch. 37. v. 24. 25.
God accounteth al iniuries done against his Church, as done against himselfe. So our Sauiour charged Saul persecuting the Church, as if he persecuted himselfe. Act. 5. 7. 4.
One cause of distrust that the people of Israel should not be restored from captiuitie, was because they were like to drie bones.
Secondly they had al generally lost their hope of restitution.
Thirdly they seemed like to trees or plants cut off at the very root.
Yet God by his power & goodnes restored them.

Before Christ ioyned the Gentils to his Church, he first vnited the two Kingdomes of Iuda and Israel: signifying that Catholikes which labour for conuersion of heretikes must first agree amongst themselues, & then their endeuours be more effectual. For so al shal sooner be made one fold vnder one shepheard.
Fulfilled by Christ the good Pastour, who bringeth al nations into one fold vnder one Pastour.
Antichrist persecuting the Church in al parts of the world, shal be resisted by some in euerie place, & at last vanquished.
Not with material fire, but with zele & feruour, Catholikes shal resist him, and finally ouercome him.
Gods people were not made captiues by the power of their enemies, as if God could not defend them, but by his permission for punishment of their sinnes.

This description of the temple & order of Priesthood, with the partition & fertilitie of the land, is much more excellent then was in Salomons time: & the new temple reedified by Zorobabel was much meaner then Salomons, & therfore this prophecie can not be vnderstood of the temple in Ierusalem, but of the Church of Christ. S. Ierome. S. Aug. l.18 c.45 ciuit.

In the wal of the Temple were interchangeably painted a Cherub signifying knowledge, & a palmetree signifying victorie, representing that they must be instructed in diuine knowledge, & so fight for victorie.
Signifying our Sauiour, who in his humanitie suffered miseries, but as a lion ouercame al enemies.

S. Ierome finding the Hebrew text & the 70. interpreters & others to differ not only in words but also in the sense, explicating as seemed to him most probable, auoucheth withal: Scio quod nescio, I know that I doe not know. For a pastour in learued faith must know what is not knowne perfectly.
The glorie of God entring at the East gate signifieth the Incarnation of Christ, which S. Thomas expoundeth also of the B. Virgin, concerning the Sonne of God.
God hath left the house of the Iewes desolate, Mat. 23. v. 38. but remaineth with the Church of Christ al dayes to the end of the world. Mat. 28. v. 20.
The perfect vnspotted Church (Eph 5. v. 27.) is only the triumphant Church.
The sacred cubite vsed in sacred things was larger then the common cubite by one palme.
The altar is called Ariel, the lion of God, because fire sometimes descending from God vpon the altar consumed the sacrifice, as a lion consumeth his praye. S. Ierome.

S. Ierome expoundeth this of the hardnes of Scripture, which no man vnderstandeth fully but the Sonne of God. Likewise of our B. Ladie a perpetual Virgin; Christ being borne of her kept her virginity without violation, as he entred the sepulcher & the house without opening, & rose from the dead without corruption.
Bands and chaynes are apt signes of captiuitie, because they are the very instruments wherewith captiues are bound.
Hangmen, or executioners, are Gods instruments, & his seruants in punishing the wicked.
It is so false that the treasures caried away shal quickly be restored, that indeed more shal be caried away before the former be recouered.
Our B. Ladie a perpetual Virgin also after the birth of Christ; as expoundeth S. Augustin, S. Ambrose, S. Chrysostome, and S. Ierome.

This astonishment and reuerence of the Prophet, and the great attention which he is admonished to haue, import the great mysteries of Christ & his Church, & not only the temple & rites of the old law, which were but figures of the new.

The land that was assigned to holy & religious purposes, & not to be alienated to priuate men nor other purposes.

Not only Christian Doctours, but also Rabbi Dauid & other Hebrewes vnderstand this prophecie of Christ the true Messias, and of the sacrifices & rites of his Church: the letter neuertheles alluding to the forme of the old law.

Al workes done by the free choise of God, that is to say done in the state of grace, doe merite eternall reward. But other good workes done in state of mortal sinne are only rewarded temporally in this world, and not in life euerlasting.

There is no historie nor probabilitie that waters issued out of the temple reedified by Zorobabel; & therfore this prophecie hath an higher & truer sense, of the Church of Christ, and the water of Baptisme.
S. Iohn saw this riuer of liuing water as clere as chrystal, proceeding from the seat of God, & of the Lambe, and the tree of life yeelding twelue fruites euerie month. Apoc. 22.
Iosephs two sonnes had ech one a whole portion, and so there were twelue tribes besides the Leuites, who had other better meanes then the rest.

By the twelue tribes of Israel S. Ierome vnderstandeth the vniuersal multitude of al glorified Saints; noting that no mention is here made of the cities of refuge, because in the glorious habitation of Saints there can be no need of refuge, where al are perfect, al secure.
As the first borne of liuing things & first fruites of al things springing, so the first portion of land allotted to Gods seruice is called the first fruites.
This description of Ierusalem agreeth not to the terrestial citie, which was nothing neere so large; and therfore al Catholike Doctours vnderstand it mystically of the Church of Christ.

The Synagogue of the Iewes being left desert, Mat. 23. v.38. Christ is with his militant Church al dayes euen to the consummation of the world. Mat.28. ult. and with his Church triumphant illuminating and glorifying it for euer and euer. Apoc.22. v.5.
Part of the holy vessels & some special persons were caried away, but the King was released at this time: for he reigned in Ierusalem eight yeares more, eleuen in al. 2. Par.36.v.5.

Nabuchodonosor had this dreame the second yeare after his great conquest of the Moabites, Ammonites, Syrians, & Ægyptians, making his Kingdome a great Monarchie; when Daniel was about the age of 35 yeares.

It is more easie to tel by the diuels help what one hath dreamed, because dreames might by some external signes be knowne vnto him: but to declare the signification which is to come is aboue the diuels or mans power.

He thought Daniel to be a litle God, subiect in power to the great God.

Sedecias being dead, and Ioachim kept in prison, there was none in state of a King amongst the Iewes: neither was there at this time anie Prophet in al Iewrie, for Daniel himselfe & Ezechiel were in Babylon.

Holy Angels doe incessantly praise God; sensible creatures doe not properly praise God because they haue not vnderstanding nor wil: but this inuitation exhorteth al men in consideration of al Gods workes to praise him as most worthie to be praised. S. Aug.
Priuations of things haue also their decent course in the vniuersal state of creatures: Darknes prayseth God, that is, bringeth forth praise in the harts of considerate men. S. Aug.
The fire burned their bands, but not their garments nor bodies: so God vseth the seruice of his creatures to giue comfort to his seruants & not torment. S. Greg.

An old and continuall malignant practise, to cal religion treason; and for that purpose to procure lawes or statutes to be enacted.

Daniel hauing accustomed to open the windowes of his vpper chamber, which gaue prospect towards Ierusalem, according to Salomons prayer 3. Reg. 8. v. 47. 48. he obserued the same custome: & was not seen publikely of manie, but his enemies searchingly found him praying.
To this miracle wrought by the power of God in defence and for the merits of this holie Prophet, S. Paul alludeth, Heb. 11. v. 33.

The law of like-punishment is so agreeable to the law of nature, that this Pagan King punished Daniels accusers, when it appeared to him that Daniel had not committed treason, but only vsed his religion and deuotion.
In order of time these two visions were before the histories written in the two precedent chapters.

Euen in the horrest persecution of Antiochus, Nero, or Antichrist, some shal constantly confesse true religion.
The God of power or strength, either Iupiter the Grecians great God, or their owne strength, wherin Antiochus & Antichrist shal trust.
His royal tabernacle or palace, betweene the dead sea & the mediterranean.

Breaking the two hornes, that is conquering the two nations of Persians & Medes.
King Alexander died when he was very yong and strong, not fully 33. yeares of age. Foure of his followers possessed euerie one a Kingdome of his Monarchie.
Antiochus Epiphanes persecuting the people of God, destroyed the sacrifice, polluted the temple, setting vp the image of Iupiter Olimpius.
One Angel demanded of another to know a thing to come.

2300 euenings and mornings, that is, 1300 dayes, which make sixe yeares, & wel neere foure months, the whole time fro the beginning of Antiochus persecution vnto his death: for he began to persecute in the yeare 143. 1. Mac. 1. v.21. & he died an. 149. 1. Mac. 6. v.16. within which time an. 148. the temple was purged 1. Mac. 4. v.52.

Ezechiel is very often called by the name of sonne of man: here also Daniel is so called by an Angel, as wel to distinguish Angelical & humane nature, as in honour of mankind, when Christ would assume: & therfore calleth himselfe by the very same title in the Ghospel.
Historically Antiochus; mystically Antichrist, as ch.12. & Mor.24.

So much as perteyned to the prophanation of the temple was fulfilled aboue 300. yeares after this prophecie: as the same is also a figure of Antichrist, it shal happen towards the end of this world. S. Greg l.30. c.12. Moral.
Assuerus, or Achasueras, not a proper name, signifieth a great Prince, or head of people.

Darius had reigned in Persia before this time, but this was the first yeare of his reigne ouer the Chaldees, and also the last, neither did he reigne a ful yeare: for Cyrus reigned some part of the seuentith yeare of the Iewes captiuitie in Babylon: in which also Baltassar was slaine.
Daniel seeing the seuentith yeare of captiuitie was come, in which God promised to deliuer his people, prayed with great zeale, & confidence for their release.
This singular deuotion with austere workes of penance & zele of his countrie merited this commendable title to be called, The man of desires.

Whereas he prayed particularly for the release of the Iewes fro captiuitie of Babylon, a farre greater thing is promised & reuealed to him, that within seuentie weekes of yeares (that is 490 yeares) the Messias Christ wil come & redeeme mankinde fro captiuitie of sinne.
Hebdomas signifying seuen, vnderstood of dayes importeth a weeke: of yeares importeth seuen yeares: so seuentie weekes of yeares are 490 yeares.

Pharao had a vision in sleepe, Gen.41. Baltassar saw a hand writing in the wal, Dan.5. but neither of them vnderstood their visions, and therfore were not Prophets. Ioseph vnderstood the former, & Daniel this other, and so they were Prophets. For as this text teacheth, vnderstanding is required that a vision be propheticall. S. Tho. 2.2. q.171. a.2.
The Angel repeateth this honourable new title to encourage him being sore frighted.

The Angel guardian of Persia. S. Ierom. S. Gregorie. This Angel for his office sake, not yet knowing Gods wil in this particular, prayed that the Iewes might remaine among the Persians for their edification and spiritual good.
Daniels proper Angel ioyned his prayers with him for the deliuerie of the Iewes from captiuitie.
S. Michael the guardian Angel of the whole Church also prayed for the same purpose.

The Angel prosecuting his speach to the Prophet sheweth that he had prayed for the King of the Persians, from the first time after the ouerthrow of Babylon, seeing him wel affected towards the Iewes, as was also the next King Cyrus, who indeed released them.

Scarcely touching other successours of Alexander, the holie Scripture here prophecieth of the Kings of Egypt on the south side of Iurie; and Syrians on the North: by whose warres the Iewes were most molested.

Euerie prophecie (saith S. Ierom. li.4.c.) before it be fulfilled is enigma, a ridle, or obscure proposition. But when it is past may more easily, sometimes certainely be interpreted. So by histories of things now past he gathereth very probably that by this King of the South was vnderstood Ptolomeus of Lagus King of Egypt.
His sonne called Ptolomeus Philadelphus excelled his father in power and magnificence.
This King of the North seemeth to be Antiochus Theo, Kings of Syria.
Ptolomeus Euergetes King of Egypt inuaded and spoyled Syria.

Seleucus and Ceraunus, and Antiochus Magnus sonnes of Seleucus Callinicus shal raise new warres against Ptolomeus Philopator King of Egypt, but the elder brother being slaine in the way, Antiochus shal prosecute the warre.
Manie Iewes deceiued by Onias flying into Egypt shal erect a temple and sacrifice, falsly auouching that they fulfill the prophecie of Isaie. ch.19. v.19.

According to the historical expositors, Antiochus Epiphanes, who liued and died basely: but mystically of Antichrist, very potent and glorious in this world, yet shal haue base beginning and an ignominious end.

This title Prince of the league or couenant, perteineth directly to Antichrist: who wil ioyne himselfe with the Iewes, pretending to obserue the law of Moyfes, and for they wil receiue him as their Messias. Ioan.5. v.41. S. Ireneus li.5. c.25.
Euen in the hottest persecution of Antiochus, Nero, or Antichrist, some shal constantly confesse true religion.
The God of power or strength, either Iupiter the Grecias great God, or their owne strength, wherin Antiochus, & Antichrist shal trust.
His royal tabernacle or palace, betweene the dead sea & the mediterranean.

S. Athanasius in Synopsi reciteth this historie in the beginning of Daniel. And S. Aug. ser.42. de tem. supposeth that Daniel about the age of twelue yeares indued with the spirit of prophecie, discouered the malicious falsehood of them, that accused Susanna.

In the transmigratiō which was made in the third yeare of Ioakim King of Iuda, the Iewes were better entreated, and had their owne iudicial tribunal, and other priuiledges, vntil the captiuitie, which happened about 19. yeares after, in the eleuenth yeare of Sedecias. At which time they were brought into much more bondage.
For more colour of iust proceeding, these wicked men gaue their false testimonie & sentence before the people.
The people gaue their opinions that she deserued death, but the false Iudges gaue sentence: For so the forme of the law required, which they pretended to fulfill. Leu.20. Deu.22.
Daniel by the guift of prophecie raysed vp the holy spirit declared that she was innocent.
Whereas the people had consented to her death, he denied his consent, & vnder tooke to conuince the false witnesses: as he did. v.14. & 58.
By this first prophetical act, Daniel began worthily to be esteemed.

This last verse of Astyages called Darius, and of Cyrus succeeding him, perteineth to the ninth chapter. And here mention is made of them, to signifie that Daniel beginning to prophecie in his childhood, continued euen to old age.
It seemeth most probable that this King was Euilmerodach, who fauoured the Iewes, and deliuered Iechonias (otherwise called Ioachim) out of prison. 4. Reg. 25. v.27.

Daniel was now about the age of 55. yeares. For being caried into Babylon at the age of tenne yeares, was there 8. yeares before Ioachim, who was there 37. yeares before he was deliuered from prison, which make in al 55.

Not only the Babylonians as is manifest in manie places, but also the Romans and most nations worshipped a serpent or dragon, a beast naturally most hating men, & most abhorred by al men. The cause of this blindnes can be no other but Gods iust punishment, suffering them for their abominable pride, and other sinnes to fal into so foolish conceits.

Although about 20 yeares before, there was no Prophet in Iurie (ch.3. v.38.) yet now this Habacuc was indued with the spirit of prophecie. As for Habacuc, the eight in order of the lesser Prophets, he prophecied before the captiuitie: yea before the Chaldees became a Monarchie. As appeareth Hab. 1. v.6.
